look.text: That jacket has more clashing colors than the palette technically allows.

give pixels.text: Three beautiful pixels! Red, green, and... whatever that one is.
Here's your frame. Factory fresh. The factory closed in 1997, but still.
It's... still warm? I have several questions.

give chicken.text: I SELL those. Aisle four. Try to keep up.

give mug.text: A mug? In THIS economy? No refunds, no mugs.

give.text: Hmm. I'll give you nothing for it. Deal? No? Suit yourself.

pickup.text: HA! Sal cannot be acquired. Many have tried.

look.text: It's a legally distinct purple appendage.

give chicken.text: I have no arms. This is just cruel.

give.text: Unless it's world domination, I don't want it.

look.text: A rubber chicken with a USB dongle in the middle. Industry standard.

use mug.text: The chicken does not fit in the mug. Worst crossover ever.

look.text: A rubber chicken full of grog. My résumé just keeps improving.
A regulation grog mug. 40% mug, 60% future holes.
Three loose pixels: red, green, and one that identifies as 'magenta-ish'.

use frame.text: Tempting, but the frame is display-only.

look.text: A certified pre-owned frame. Only dropped once.
A semicolon. The difference between shipping and a syntax error.

then.sub: or: The Secret of the Monkey Island Build Server

then.text: Ugh. One minute I'm filing bug #1337 — 'game engine eats QA tester' —
...and the next, I'm INSIDE the engine. The bug report writes itself. From within.
Restart the Main Loop to escape. The boiler requires: fuel, a fresh frame, and terminating punctuation.
Fuel, frame, semicolon. How hard can it be?

look.text: The RENDER pipe and the AUDIO pipe. Do NOT cross the streams.

pull.text: The pipes are hard-coded. Literally. Someone wrote them in pen.

use.text: I'd need a plumber. Wrong franchise.

open.text: The firebox swings open with a dramatic creak. Nice foley work.

look.text: The Main Loop boiler. Cold as a code freeze.

use chicken_grog.text: I should open the firebox first. Even grog respects encapsulation.

use semicolon.text: The firebox is closed. Punctuation can't fix everything.

use frame.text: Firebox first. Then frame. Order of operations.

look.text: The firebox is open. It hungers for fuel, frames, and punctuation.

use chicken_grog.text: GLUG. GLUG. GLUG. The boiler burps politely.
The chicken survives. The chicken always survives.

use semicolon.text: The semicolon slots into the infinite loop. Somewhere, a while(true) finally rests.

use frame.text: The fresh frame primes the render pump. Smells like new tab.

use mug.text: The boiler drinks grog, not mugs. It has standards.

use chicken.text: An EMPTY chicken? The boiler is insulted.

pickup.text: It weighs as much as technical debt.

actions.text: Gauge reads 100%. A round number. Suspicious, but I'll take it.
The needle twitched! For this engine, that's practically a sprint.
The needle reads ZERO. Same as my frame budget.

look.text: The Master Lever. The label says 'SHIP IT'. The subtext says 'please'.

push.text: It's a PULL lever. The engine is pedantic about input methods.

then.text: We shipped. It's over. Please, no sequels.

of.text: CRITICAL: MainLoop not ready. Have you tried grog?
Nothing. The boiler wants its three offerings: fuel, frame, punctuation.
A distant voice whispers: 'works on my machine'.

look.text: Employee of the Month: the LOOK AT verb. Nine months running.

push.text: Behind the poster: solid wall. This isn't that kind of game. ...Is it? No. It is not.

look.text: A grog mug. The pinnacle of disposable container technology.

pickup.text: One regulation grog mug. What could possibly go wrong?

look.text: The shelf misses its mug already.
'COOLANT', says the label. 'GROG XD', says the barrel below. The barrel is winning.

use.text: I need a container. Preferably one that won't dissolve.

open.text: Not without a container. I've seen what this stuff does to floors.

use mug.text: The grog dissolves the mug in 1.3 seconds.
A new personal record, notes the barrel.

use chicken.text: The rubber chicken fills with grog and shows no signs of dissolving.
Finally. A use for this thing. Somewhere, Ron Gilbert nods.

use chicken_grog.text: It's already full. Greed is a bug, not a feature.

look.text: GROG XD — the extra-dissolvent blend. Now with 20% more X.

open.text: The barrel is sealed by union rules.

look.text: A toolbox. Property of the Build Engineer. Missing since the Great Refactor.
Inside: one rubber chicken, one USB dongle, zero tools. Checks out.

pickup.text: A rubber chicken with a USB dongle in the middle. Finally, some real engineering.

look.text: Empty, except for a sticker: 'WORKS ON MY MACHINE'.
A bug. It's been in the engine since 1990. At this point it's load-bearing.

pickup.text: It's not a feature yet. Give it time.

talk.text: The bug files a report about YOU.

look.text: A structural walk-behind column.
(You can walk behind it. This cost three entire lines of code.)
Outside: the skybox. It's a JPEG of a nicer game.

open.text: And let the z-fighting in? No thanks.

look.text: The shipping door. Beyond it: the Asset Dock, where content goes to be monetized.
A hatch overgrown with vines. The air smells of... nested conversation.

then.text: The Asset Dock. Everything the engine loads ships through here.
This room SCROLLS, by the way. Very expensive feature. Try to look impressed.

look.text: Back to the Engine Room, where the magic segfaults.
Crates labeled 'SPRITES, ASSORTED, SLIGHTLY DEFECTIVE' and 'TODO: art'.

open.text: Nailed shut. The nails are also assets. Sal counted them.

look.text: The label reads: 'MISC VILLAIN, PURPLE, ONE (1)'. It's... breathing?

open.text: FREEDOM! ...This is still the dock, isn't it.
The crate had a tentacle. Of course the crate had a tentacle.

look.text: An open crate. The packing peanuts are tiny compressed textures.
The water is one gradient op and one scatter op. Very cost-effective. Very wet.

use.text: Swim in unallocated memory? Hard pass.

look.text: The good ship Dependency. She hasn't shipped in years.
It's just a white ellipse with a gray ellipse on it. Gorgeous.
'SAL'S SLIGHTLY USED ASSETS — Every pixel has a past.'
A sunny path to Boundary Beach, where the map ends and the lawyers begin.

then.text: Boundary Beach. Walk toward the horizon and watch me shrink.
It's called 'the scale band'. It's cheaper than 3D and twice as funny.

look.text: From here I can see the skybox seam. Don't tell the art director.
The sea. Endless, blue, and clipped at exactly y=92.

use.text: I'd swim, but my collision box can't.

look.text: A palm tree. You can walk behind it. Depth sorting: the unsung hero.

push.text: A coconut declines to fall. It's rigged. Literally — no physics engine.

look.text: A rock. Suspiciously pushable-looking.

push.text: It moved! Underneath: three loose pixels and one very surprised crab function.

pickup.text: It's a rock, not an asset. Well. It IS an asset. But no.

look.text: The rock, relocated. It seems happier here.
Three loose pixels, still warm from the framebuffer.

pickup.text: Three pixels. I'm rich in the worst possible currency.

look.text: A note inside reads: 'HELP, I'M TRAPPED IN A GAME ENGINE FACTORY.' ...Relatable.

pickup.text: The bottle is load-bearing. The beach would collapse. Trust me, I'm QA.

open.text: The cork is a compressed texture. It won't budge.

look.text: The legendary Invisible Wall. The engine's oldest employee.

push.text: The wall doesn't move. The wall has tenure.

talk.text: Any comment on the edge of the world?
The wall declines to comment. It is, after all, just four walk polygon vertices.

look.text: Back to the Asset Dock.

then.text: A grove. And in the middle... a tree with a FACE.
I am the DIALOG TREE. All conversation flows through my branches.
Of course it talks. Of course it does.

look.text: A Dialog Tree. Every conversation in the industry was picked from one of these.

use.text: You don't 'use' a Dialog Tree. You ENDURE it.

push.text: Rude. I have deep roots and deeper grudges.

pickup.text: I am NOT an inventory item. I'm a data structure with feelings.

look.text: A gleaming semicolon, nestled in the roots.

pickup.text: One semicolon, slightly used. I will guard it with my life.

look.text: Save points. Obsolete since autosave. They're bitter about it.

pickup.text: They hiss: 'F5 killed our whole industry.' I back away slowly.

talk.text: The mushrooms reminisce about 1997 in tiny, bitter voices.

look.text: The canopy glows with option-fruits. Some are grayed out. Requirements not met.
Back to the Engine Room.

actions.text: Only the finest certified pre-owned assets! Sprites with one careful owner!
Idle animations, barely idled! And today only: a gently used walk cycle. It limps, but that's CHARACTER.

options.text: I need a fresh frame for the render pump.

actions.text: A FRESH frame? Ooh. Pricey. Three pixels, non-negotiable.
Loose ones, mind you. I don't take framebuffer credit.

options.text: Where do I find loose pixels?

actions.text: Boundary Beach, past the dock. Pixels wash up under the rocks.
Dead pixels float. Live ones hide. Bring a rock-pushing attitude.
Robbery? These are ARTISANAL pixels. Hand-placed. Locally sourced.
Fine. Three pixels and I throw in a free compliment: you have LOVELY collision bounds.
The Asset Dock! Everything the engine loads ships through here.
Twenty years on this dock, and the crates STILL say 'TODO: art'.
Come back anytime! Sal's assets never depreciate! ...Legally I must add: they do.

options.text: Hi. I'm Deb. I need a semicolon.

actions.text: A semicolon! My rarest fruit. It hangs from my highest branch.
Defeat me in TALK COMBAT and it falls. Insults and comebacks. Classic rules.
I have more branches than your git repository, small tester. Prepare yourself.
It's a 9-slice. Don't touch it.
Under my roots. Take it and go, before I think of a comeback.
Make like YOU, and leave. Wait. That's not how the joke goes.
ROUND ONE! You fight like a QA tester!

options.text: And you're about as sharp as a rounded corner.

actions.text: ...Acceptable. ROUND TWO approaches.
Devastating. My sap runs cold. Try again.

options.text: My mother was a text parser!

actions.text: I know. Lovely woman. Terrible grammar. AGAIN!
ROUND TWO! My canopy has better coverage than your unit tests!

options.text: Coverage means nothing when half your branches are dead code.

actions.text: OOF. Right in the heartwood. FINAL ROUND!
Neither can you, apparently. AGAIN!

options.text: That's... that's not even an insult!

actions.text: Neither is that. Round two continues!
FINAL ROUND! I've been version-controlled since before you were a feature request!

options.text: Then it's time somebody pruned the main branch.

actions.text: HA! HAHAHA! *creak* FINE! FINE! You win!
One semicolon, as promised. It fell off a for-loop in 1993. Cherish it.
Compelling. But no. AGAIN!

options.text: I... uh... your leaves are ugly?

actions.text: My leaves are PROCEDURAL. Round three continues!

options.text: Are you okay? You were in a crate.

actions.text: Okay? OKAY? I've been in shipping since 1993!
The label says 'MISC VILLAIN'. I am NOT misc. I am PREMIUM villain.
I get that a lot. I was ALMOST a star, once. Then the rights lawyers came.
Now I'm 'legally distinct'. It says so on my tag.

options.text: What do you want out of life?

actions.text: World domination. Or a speaking role. At this point, honestly? Either.
TREMBLE BEFORE ME! ...Please visit again. It gets lonely on the dock.

ending.text: Okay. Semicolon: installed. Frame: primed. Grog: ...chickened.
Somewhere, a build pipeline turns green for the first time in years.

ending.sub: (in a cozy, WebAudio sort of way)

ending.text: It compiles! IT COMPILES! Quick, nobody touch anything!

ending.sub: A Grog Engine demo — 100% JSON, 0% binary assets

ending.text: Deb was promoted to Senior Semicolon Architect.
The tentacle got his spin-off. It was cancelled after one episode.
Sal sold this ending to three other games.

ending.sub: Made with Grog Studio — now go make your own

C4 E4 G4 C5 . G4 C5 E5 . C5 E5 G5 C5 . G4 . A4 C5 E5 A5 . E5 A5 C5 G4 B4 D5 G5 C5 . . .
x . x x x . x . x . x x x . x x

look: I see nothing special. And I'm a trained observer.
Yep. That's definitely one of the objects of all time.
pickup: I don't need it. Probably. ...No, I don't need it.
That asset is still under license.
use: Nope. Not even with duct tape.
open: Access denied. It's not even locked, it's just rude.
close: It's as closed as it gets.
push: I push. Nothing happens. Story of my career.
talk: It has nothing to say. Lucky thing.
I talk. It doesn't. Classic one-sided relationship.
